commands.review_summary:
The PR appends active provider usage-window summaries to `/usage tokens` and `/usage full` reply footers, adds regression tests, and documents the new footer behavior.

PR surface: Source +97, Tests +88, Docs +5. Total +190 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main lacks the quota-footer bridge and the PR's timeout issue is visible from the loader contract plus the changed reply-runner call path; I did not run a live reply in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR needs contributor-visible follow-up for the timeout blocker and missing real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(usage): show provider quota in usage footer
target.body:
﻿Fixes #7379

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users who enable `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` would only see per-response token/cost details and still need to run `/status` separately to see active-provider quota windows such as GitHub Copilot Premium/Chat usage.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change reuses the existing provider-usage summary and formatter seams to append a compact active-provider quota summary to the reply usage footer when available. Provider fetching stays plugin-owned, footer loading is timeout-bounded and omit-on-failure, and cost/session footer behavior remains limited to the existing `/usage full` path.

## User Impact

Users can now see provider quota windows directly in each `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` reply footer when their active provider exposes them. If credentials are missing or the usage endpoint is unavailable, replies still show the normal token/cost footer without failing.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`: passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/models.test.ts`: passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.ts docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`: passed
- `git diff --check`: passed

AI-assisted: I used AI assistance to inspect the relevant reply-footer and provider-usage code paths, draft the change, and choose focused validation. I reviewed the final diff and understand the submitted change.
